commands.review_summary:
The PR updates native prompt image loading and the direct image understanding tool so `media://inbound/...` is not rewritten as a workspace path, and adds native loader regression coverage for managed inbound URI hydration.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +30. Total +36 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main rewrites `media://inbound/...` through workspace path handling in the native loader and direct image tool before the media-store hydrator can run. I did not execute tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor-provided real behavior proof; no safe automated repair is currently identified.

target.title: fix(media): resolve inbound media refs consistently
target.body:
Summary
- Resolve native prompt-image media refs through the managed media reference helper before workspace path resolution.
- Keep direct image tool `media://inbound/...` refs as media-store URIs so `loadWebMedia` hydrates the managed inbound file instead of constructing `workspace/media:/inbound/...`.
- Add regression coverage for native loader managed inbound URI hydration.

Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts src/media/media-reference.test.ts src/media/web-media.test.ts`

Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Telegram/channel inbound images saved under the managed media store and referenced as `media://inbound/<id>` now load in both native prompt-image loading and the direct image understanding tool instead of being workspace-resolved as `media:/inbound` paths.
Real environment tested: local macOS checkout, Node/Vitest via `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`, with real temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/media/inbound/*.png` files.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts src/media/media-reference.test.ts src/media/web-media.test.ts`.
Evidence after fix: `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.test.ts` creates `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/media/inbound/telegram-photo.png`, calls `loadImageFromRef()` with `media://inbound/telegram-photo.png` and `workspaceOnly: true`, and receives the PNG image bytes; the existing direct image tool managed inbound test also succeeds with `workspaceOnly: true`.
Observed result after fix: 6 test files passed, 295 tests passed.
What was not tested: live Telegram delivery was not exercised in this local proof; the filesystem media-store path and direct/native agent loading behavior were verified with real local files.

Fixes #87024.

If this PR is squash/reworked, please preserve author attribution or include:
Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>
